This classification places Ciminalis squarrosa within the larger family of dogbane or oleander plants. The Apocynaceae family encompasses a diverse range of plants, including periwinkle, milkweed, and dogbane. Ciminalis squarrosa's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within this family.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as having antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
